title: Primary Experimental Feedback on a Co-manipulated Robotic System for Assisted Cervical Surgery

new_ratings.score_reason: Very preliminary feasibility study with minimal sample size, no control condition, and insufficient depth for meaningful scientific contribution
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a relevant clinical problem but provides no new insights beyond basic feasibility demonstration with a commercial collaborative robot
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Only 15 drillings, no statistical analysis, no control group, no comparison to existing systems, incomplete system description
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Co-manipulation for cervical drilling has some novelty but the approach uses off-the-shelf components without novel algorithmic or control contributions
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Paper is understandable but contains grammatical errors, formatting issues, and lacks critical methodological details

title: Electrochemical stability and lithium insertion at the Li|Li3OCl solid electrolyte interface

new_ratings.score_reason: Incremental DFT study on a previously examined interface system with standard methods, limited novelty, and presentation issues
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a relevant problem but findings are largely confirmatory and confined to one material system without broader design principles
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Standard DFT methodology applied reasonably but with notable gaps in k-point convergence testing, functional accuracy, and incomplete analysis of unstable configurations
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The Li|Li3OCl interface has been studied before by other groups and partially by the same authors; the layer-by-layer insertion analysis adds modest new information
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Paper contains grammatical errors, inconsistent notation, formatting issues, and some results are insufficiently detailed or explained

title: Birth, Death, and Replication at Surfaces: Universal Laws of Autocatalytic Dynamics

new_ratings.score_reason: Elegant theoretical framework bridging two established fields, but limited by qualitative rather than quantitative results and deferred detailed analysis
new_ratings.significance_reason: Fills a genuine gap between diffusion-controlled reactions and branching processes with broad potential applications across physics, chemistry, and biology
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Mathematical derivations are sound and general, but quantitative analysis is minimal with only one numerical example and key scaling results stated without proof
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The conceptual extension of diffusion-controlled reactions to include surface-mediated branching with nonlinear Robin boundary conditions is genuinely novel
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured presentation with clear physical intuition, good schematic figure, and logical progression from integral to PDE formulation

title: Spin-adapted neural network backflow for strongly correlated electrons

new_ratings.score_reason: A significant methodological advance that solves a recognized problem (spin contamination in NQS) with practical demonstrations on challenging chemical systems, though limited by simple NN architecture and energy not yet competitive with best available estimates
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the critical bottleneck of spin symmetry in neural network quantum states for strongly correlated chemistry, with demonstrated advantages over both NNBF and SA-DMRG on FeMoco
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Systematic benchmarking with appropriate baselines and fair parameter comparisons, though lacking wall-clock timing comparisons and deeper optimization landscape analysis
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel integration of spin adaptation into second-quantized NNBF with original contributions in tensor compression and particle-hole duality, building on concurrent first-quantization work
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear mathematical exposition, informative figures, and logical progression from theory to benchmarks to applications

title: MiDAS: A Multimodal Data Acquisition System and Dataset for Robot-Assisted Minimally Invasive Surgery

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid infrastructure contribution addressing a real community need, but limited by small datasets, partial validation on clinical platforms, and modest downstream task performance
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine practical bottleneck in surgical robotics research with open-source tools and datasets that could enable broader participation
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Reasonable validation methodology but ground-truth comparison only on Raven-II, small dataset sizes, and the HandKP modality has severe reliability issues
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Integrates known sensing modalities into a unified system rather than introducing fundamentally new techniques; the system engineering is practical but not conceptually novel
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures, comprehensive appendices, and thorough experimental reporting; taxonomy and annotation procedures are well-documented

title: Nonequilibrium energy transport in driven-dissipative quantum systems

new_ratings.score_reason: Technically correct methodological contribution with limited novelty; the main insight follows straightforwardly from standard rotating frame transformations
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses energy transport in driven systems but the practical advance over existing Floquet methods is modest, and no new physics is discovered
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Solid validation against Floquet master equation, but limited to weak coupling and specific symmetry conditions without systematic error analysis
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The rotating frame approach and Born-Markov derivation are standard; the key observation about driving phase in transition rates is straightforward once the transformation is applied
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally readable but contains grammatical errors, inconsistent notation (dDME vs dQME), and could better articulate what is genuinely new versus standard methodology

title: CT-FineBench: A Diagnostic Fidelity Benchmark for Fine-Grained Evaluation of CT Report Generation

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses a real evaluation gap with a reasonable approach, but limited by questionable clinical validation, recall-only design, and inconsistent correlation improvements
new_ratings.significance_reason: Identifies an important problem in CT report evaluation and proposes a structured solution, but practical impact limited by recall-only nature and dataset constraints
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Sensitivity analysis is well-designed, but human annotation lacks inter-annotator agreement, no radiologist involvement, modest correlation study, and no significance tests
new_ratings.novelty_reason: QA-based evaluation is established in general NLP; the contribution is mainly in applying and adapting it to CT reports with a clinical attribute schema
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally well-structured with clear figures and tables, but some redundancy in writing and insufficient detail on annotation quality

title: Reed--Muller Codes Achieve the Symmetric Capacity on Finite-State Channels

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong theoretical contribution extending RM capacity-achieving results to channels with memory, though limited by MAP decoding assumption and symbol-error metric
new_ratings.significance_reason: Important theoretical advance in showing structured codes achieve capacity on FSCs, but practical impact limited by computational intractability of MAP decoding
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Mathematically rigorous proof with careful handling of mixing, coupling, rate preservation, and parameter selection; all conditions systematically verified
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel combination of existing techniques (capacity-via-symmetry, blocking, interleaving, RM automorphism properties) applied to FSCs; individual components are largely known
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear three-component structure; the informal theorem statement and proof outline in the introduction effectively guide the reader

title: User-Centric Object Navigation: A Benchmark with Integrated User Habits for Personalized Embodied Object Search

new_ratings.score_reason: Introduces a meaningful new benchmark direction but limited by minimal methodological novelty, small-scale experiments, and synthetic-only habit generation
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real gap in personalized embodied navigation, but impact is bounded by the focus on habit utilization only and limited ecological validity
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Reasonable experimental comparisons but small scene pool (22 scenes), qualitative-only real-world evaluation, and missing ablation studies on key parameters
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel problem formulation and benchmark, but the proposed HRM is a standard RAG retrieval step with minimal technical innovation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and illustrative case studies, though some experimental details are underspecified

title: Tight Inapproximability for Welfare-Maximizing Autobidding Equilibria

new_ratings.score_reason: Definitive resolution of a natural and important open problem with tight bounds and comprehensive extensions
new_ratings.significance_reason: Closes the gap between PoA bounds and computational hardness in a practically important setting, with implications for billion-dollar advertising markets
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Technically rigorous reduction with complete proofs, careful gadget composition, and thorough treatment of edge cases including approximate equilibria
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel gadget construction and reduction architecture, though the high-level approach of reducing from label cover is standard in hardness of approximation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with excellent overview figure, clear problem formulation, and logical progression from basic gadgets to main results

title: Adiabatic Ramsey Interferometry for Measuring Weak Nonlinearities with Super-Heisenberg Precision

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid theoretical extension of existing framework to nonlinearity estimation with clear SH scaling results, but incremental over prior work and lacking experimental grounding
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a relevant problem in trapped ion quantum technologies with practical advantages, but the impact is limited by being purely theoretical and building closely on prior methods
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Clean analytical framework with numerical validation, but limited noise analysis, no systematic study of adiabaticity breakdown, and perturbative assumptions not rigorously bounded
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Extension of the authors' prior adiabatic sensing technique to nonlinear couplings; the application is new but the core methodology is established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and logical flow; notation is standard though some derivation steps are compressed

title: Quantum mutual information, coherence and unified relations of top quarks in QCD processes

new_ratings.score_reason: Straightforward application of known quantum information measures to known QCD density matrices with limited new physical insight
new_ratings.significance_reason: Results are either trivial consequences of known properties or expected from general principles; no new predictions or experimental guidance
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Calculations appear correct but limited to LO QCD with no uncertainty estimates; trivial results are overstated
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Largely a computational exercise applying existing tools to existing states; prior work by Afik et al. already established this framework
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Figures are clear but writing contains grammatical errors and the paper lacks a sharp central message distinguishing it from prior work

title: ChemFit: A concurrent framework for model parametrization

new_ratings.score_reason: Useful engineering contribution addressing a genuine need, but limited novelty, modest demonstrations, and lack of comparison to existing tools reduce impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real practical problem in force field parameterization, but the contribution is primarily software engineering with limited scientific novelty
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Case studies are illustrative but lack optimizer details, scaling analysis, convergence studies, and comparison to alternative approaches
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The software design is clean but the underlying concepts (objective function decomposition, concurrent evaluation) are not new; similar functionality exists in various forms
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-written with clear figures and good explanation of the concurrency model, though missing important methodological details about the optimization runs

title: Chirality transfer in nematic liquid crystals via octahedral coordination compounds with Δ and Λ chirality

new_ratings.score_reason: A competent review of a niche topic consolidating known results, but no new data, not peer-reviewed, and limited broader impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an interesting but niche problem with persistent practical barriers that limit real-world adoption
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Cited work uses sound methods and the theoretical framework is well-applied, though quantitative prediction remains elusive and the review is not peer-reviewed
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Review article presenting no new data or frameworks; value is in organization and synthesis of existing literature, much of it the author's own
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear figures, logical progression from fundamentals to applications, and accessible writing for the target audience

title: NS-RGS: Newton-Schulz based Riemannian gradient method for orthogonal group synchronization

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid technical contribution with rigorous theory but modest practical impact and a gap between GPU-efficiency claims and CPU-only experiments
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real computational bottleneck but the speedup is modest (~2×) and the practical impact is incremental rather than transformative
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Thorough leave-one-out analysis with near-optimal noise thresholds; detailed proofs tracking inexact retraction errors through the convergence argument
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel application of Newton-Schulz to group synchronization with inexact retraction theory, but the individual components (NS iterations, leave-one-out analysis) are well-established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear algorithm descriptions, though the proof sections are dense with many constants and the experimental section could be more comprehensive

title: Neural Aided Kalman Filtering for UAV State Estimation in Degraded Sensing Environments

new_ratings.score_reason: Incremental combination of existing techniques with synthetic-only evaluation and limited baselines; useful but not sufficiently validated for strong impact claims
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real problem but the contribution is a straightforward combination of BNN and Kalman filtering without deep insight; limited to degraded-sensing scenarios where it helps
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Synthetic-only evaluation, unfair baseline comparison (trained BNN vs. untuned process models), no comparison with other neural-Kalman methods, limited ablation studies
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Using variational BNN uncertainty in Kalman covariance propagation is a modest but identifiable distinction from prior neural-Kalman approaches; the combination is largely engineering-level
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and metric definitions; formulation is accessible though some details on training are sparse

title: EgoVerse: An Egocentric Human Dataset for Robot Learning from Around the World

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong dataset and infrastructure contribution with cross-lab validation, but limited algorithmic exploration and reliance on offline metrics for key findings
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a fundamental data scalability bottleneck in robot learning with a platform designed for lasting community impact
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Multi-lab robot evaluation is commendable, but diversity studies use only offline metrics and a single policy architecture limits generalizability of conclusions
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The consortium and living-dataset concept is novel for this domain, but the algorithmic approach (BC co-training, flow matching) is largely assembled from existing techniques
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures and thorough appendix, though the industry data component lacks transparency compared to the academic portion

title: A Lightweight Universal Machine-Learning Interatomic Potential via Knowledge Distillation for Scalable Atomistic Simulations

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong practical contribution combining knowledge distillation with comprehensive validation across diverse applications, though the approach is relatively straightforward and accuracy gaps remain
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real and growing bottleneck in scaling universal MLIPs to large systems, with demonstrated value in industrially relevant applications
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Comprehensive benchmarking across diverse tasks with proper baselines, though missing comparisons with some recent models and deeper analysis of failure modes
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Knowledge distillation for MLIPs has precedent (LightPFP); the contribution is more in the systematic execution and breadth of validation than in methodological innovation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures and thorough description of methods, applications, and results; easy to follow despite the breadth of content

title: LiquiLM: Bridging the Semantic Gap in Liquidity Flaw Audit via DCN and LLMs

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses a relevant problem with a reasonable architecture, but methodological concerns around synthetic data, limited reproducibility, and incomplete real-world validation temper the impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Targets a genuine and growing security need in DeFi/PoL ecosystems with practical CVE discoveries, though the broader applicability beyond this niche is uncertain
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Ablation studies are well-designed, but AI-completed datasets, undisclosed CVE details, missing sensitivity analyses, and lack of ground truth for real-world experiments undermine confidence
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The combination of DCN pre-filtering with structured LLM prompting for liquidity flaws is novel in application domain, but the individual techniques are established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally well-structured with clear figures and algorithms, though the paper conflates PoL-specific and general contributions and some design choices lack justification

title: Utilitarian Distortion Under Probabilistic Voting

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong theoretical contribution resolving a known tension in the distortion framework, with near-tight bounds and broad relevance to both social choice and AI alignment
new_ratings.significance_reason: Resolves a longstanding counterintuitive result in distortion analysis and provides theoretical grounding for Borda/Copeland use in RLHF preference aggregation
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Near-tight upper and lower bounds for multiple voting rules, careful technical analysis of logistic sigmoid properties, and clean proof structures throughout
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Builds directly on recent work (Gölz et al. 2025, Goyal & Sarmasakar 2025) with improved bounds and new rule analyses; the conceptual framework is inherited but results are substantially new
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized with clear summary table and proof sketches, though some proofs (e.g., Copeland upper bound) could benefit from more intuitive explanation of the case structure

title: DAIRE: A lightweight AI model for real-time detection of Controller Area Network attacks in the Internet of Vehicles

new_ratings.score_reason: Incremental contribution with a trivial architectural formula, near-perfect results on easy benchmarks, and unfair hardware comparisons that collectively limit scientific impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a relevant problem but offers marginal improvement over saturated prior work on well-studied datasets
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Structured evaluation with cross-validation but undermined by unfair hardware comparisons, lack of embedded deployment, and no adversarial testing
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The neuron allocation formula N_i = i × c is trivial and lacks theoretical justification; remaining components are standard ML practice
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures and tables, though the writing overstates contributions and includes unnecessary background material

title: A Comparative Study of Hybrid Quantum and Classical Genetic Algorithms in Portfolio Optimization

new_ratings.score_reason: Applies an existing algorithm to a trivially small problem with insufficient baselines and statistical rigor to support its claims
new_ratings.significance_reason: The 9-asset binary problem is too small to demonstrate meaningful advantages, and no novel algorithmic contribution is made
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Lacks statistical tests, proper baselines, scalability analysis, and clarity on whether quantum hardware was actually used
new_ratings.novelty_reason: No new algorithm or methodology; purely an application of an existing HQGA to a standard toy problem
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized and clearly written with good background coverage, though missing critical experimental details

title: Security and Resilience in Autonomous Vehicles: A Proactive Design Approach

new_ratings.score_reason: A practical but incremental contribution that applies established security techniques to AV systems without significant methodological novelty or rigorous evaluation against sophisticated threats
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important problem but the solutions are well-known techniques applied to a new domain without advancing the state of the art meaningfully
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Physical platform experiments are a plus, but narrow attack scenarios, modest detection accuracy, simplistic threat models, and no comparison to existing AV security frameworks weaken the evaluation
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Individual components (ML classifiers, SHA-256 hashing, redundancy/fallback) are standard techniques; their combination in an AV context is practical but not novel
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized as a book chapter with clear figures and tables, though the writing is sometimes repetitive and the mathematical formulation of anomaly detection adds little beyond standard definitions

title: Real Life Is Uncertain. Consensus Should Be Too!

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong conceptual contribution with compelling quantitative examples, but limited by the gap between the ambitious vision and the simplified analysis delivered; no concrete protocol or system
new_ratings.significance_reason: Identifies a genuine paradigm mismatch with practical cost/sustainability implications, though the ultimate impact depends on follow-up work delivering concrete protocols
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Analysis is correct but relies on strong simplifying assumptions (independence, static probabilities, small N) that contradict the paper's own motivating arguments about correlated, dynamic failures
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The bridging of storage reliability reasoning to consensus is a useful reframing; however, probabilistic quorums and failure-aware consensus have been explored before, and no new protocol is proposed
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured and clearly written with effective use of concrete examples and tables; the argument flows logically from motivation through analysis to vision

title: Graph Transformer-Based Pathway Embedding for Cancer Prognosis

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid methodological contribution with a novel gene embedding strategy, but limited by absence of external validation, incomplete baselines, and retrospective-only biological validation
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real gap in biologically-informed deep learning by introducing patient-conditioned gene embeddings and pathway rewiring analysis, with potential for broad applicability
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Well-described architecture and reasonable cross-validation, but no external validation, missing key baselines (Pathformer, P-NET), no statistical significance tests between methods, and small metastatic sample sizes
new_ratings.novelty_reason: FiLM-based gene conditioning is a genuinely novel approach in this domain; pathway rewiring analysis adds interpretability beyond existing methods
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Thorough mathematical description and clear figures, but paper is lengthy and the extensive literature validation tables may overstate the strength of biological evidence

title: On the Suboptimality of Rate--Distortion-Optimal Compression: Fundamental Accuracy Limits for Distributed Localization

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong theoretical contribution with a clear and impactful message, but limited in scope by idealized assumptions and lack of achievability results
new_ratings.significance_reason: Demonstrates a fundamental and practically important misalignment between RD-optimal compression and localization, with broad implications for ISAC and sensor networks
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Mathematically sound derivations with clean closed-form results, though limited to Gaussian models and a single numerical example
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel combination of RD test-channel models with spectral Fisher information for localization, though individual components are well-established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Excellent exposition for a dense theoretical letter; physical intuition is clearly conveyed alongside rigorous mathematics

title: Entropy Centroids as Intrinsic Rewards for Test-Time Scaling

new_ratings.score_reason: Practical and well-validated method for test-time scaling with broad applicability, but moderate novelty and lacking theoretical depth
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a timely and important problem in LLM deployment with a solution that works across diverse tasks and scales
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Comprehensive experiments across many models and tasks with good ablations, but missing reward model comparisons and formal theoretical analysis
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The HEP formalization and centroid concept are creative repackaging of entropy analysis; the core insight that early uncertainty is better is relatively intuitive
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-written paper with excellent visualizations, clear method description, and informative case studies in the appendix

title: Thermal Entanglement and Out-of-Equilibrium Thermodynamics in 1D Bose gases

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid analytical results with clear experimental relevance, but methodology is largely a combination of established techniques applied to a specific (Gaussian) setting
new_ratings.significance_reason: Useful entanglement certification strategy and connection to thermodynamics, but impact is limited by the Gaussian restriction and relatively narrow scope
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Thorough analytical and numerical treatment with multiple cross-validations, though quasi-adiabatic and thermalization analyses are less complete
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Individual methods are well-established; novelty lies in their combination and the specific analytical result of the two-mode witness structure
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear physical intuition, informative boxes, and detailed appendices, though some sections are dense

title: Realtime-VLA V2: Learning to Run VLAs Fast, Smooth, and Accurate

new_ratings.score_reason: Practical and timely systems contribution addressing a real deployment bottleneck, but lacks quantitative evaluation, ablations, and comparisons with prior work
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important and underexplored problem (faster-than-demonstration VLA execution) with practical relevance for commercial robotics deployment
new_ratings.rigor_reason: No success rate tables, no ablation studies, no comparison with competing methods, and key model training details are underspecified
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel integration of known components for VLA deployment; the human-in-the-loop speed modulation concept is interesting but individual techniques are established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-written with clear problem decomposition and good figures; the step-by-step framework presentation is easy to follow despite some vague implementation details

title: Ad Insertion in LLM-Generated Responses

new_ratings.score_reason: Practically motivated framework for an important problem, but limited in theoretical novelty and empirical validation depth
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the commercially critical problem of LLM monetization with a deployable architecture, but lacks real-world validation
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Theoretical results are correct but straightforward applications of VCG; empirical studies are small-scale and lack end-to-end real-world evaluation
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The genre-based decoupling concept is a clean design contribution, but the technical components (VCG, LLM-as-a-Judge, embeddings) are existing tools assembled together
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and good exposition; the matrix decomposition perspective is particularly intuitive

title: Toward Secure Web to ERP Payment Flows: A Case Study of HTTP Header Trust Failures in SAP Based Systems

new_ratings.score_reason: A practitioner-oriented experience report with no novel technical contribution, no formal analysis, and recommendations that restate well-known security principles
new_ratings.significance_reason: The vulnerability class described is already well-documented in OWASP guidelines and security textbooks; no new insight is generated
new_ratings.rigor_reason: No formal methods, no experiments, no empirical data; the case study is abstracted to the point of being unfalsifiable
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The vulnerability pattern (trusting client-side state for payment decisions) is canonical in web security; no new technique, tool, or formal model is introduced
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized and clearly written, but the extreme abstraction of the case study undermines the informational content

title: From GDSII to Wafer: EDA Design Flow and Data Conversion for Wafer-Scale Manufacturing of Superconducting Quantum Chips

new_ratings.score_reason: Useful problem framing and systematic overview, but functions as a position/survey paper with minimal experimental validation and limited technical novelty
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real and timely industrial need in quantum chip manufacturing, but the contribution is more organizational than technical
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Prototype validation is superficial (two data points, no fabrication validation, no defect detection analysis), and DRC thresholds lack rigorous justification
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Primarily adapts well-established CMOS EDA concepts to the quantum domain without introducing fundamentally new algorithms or techniques
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and tables; the data-flow architecture is presented logically, though some sections are more descriptive than analytical

title: Distributional Robustness of Linear Contracts

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong theoretical contribution with elegant novel proof technique and comprehensive treatment across multiple settings, though informational assumptions are debatable
new_ratings.significance_reason: Provides a foundational distributional robustness justification for linear contracts, complementing Carroll (2015) and extending to multi-party settings with practical tractability results
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Clean proof architecture with well-structured steps, though some assumptions (compactness, mean knowledge) could be more thoroughly discussed and some edge cases deserve additional treatment
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The concavification approach via self-inducing actions is a genuinely new proof technique that elegantly handles multi-dimensional settings where prior methods fail
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized with clear exposition of main ideas and good use of geometric intuition, though some extensions could benefit from more detailed discussion

title: Ghost in the Agent: Redefining Information Flow Tracking for LLM Agents

new_ratings.score_reason: Novel and timely framework addressing a real gap, but limited by self-constructed benchmark and single baseline comparison
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a critical emerging security problem in LLM agent systems with a practical, deployable solution
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Well-structured formalism but weakened by author-constructed benchmark, single baseline, and uncalibrated LLM-based causal confidence scores
new_ratings.novelty_reason: First systematic adaptation of taint analysis to LLM agents with a well-motivated three-class taxonomy and provenance graph design
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Exceptionally clear writing with well-chosen motivating examples, formal definitions, and systematic presentation of the framework

title: TimelyFreeze: Adaptive Parameter Freezing Mechanism for Pipeline Parallelism

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid systems contribution with clean formulation and thorough experiments, but limited scale and scope constrain broader impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real inefficiency in pipeline parallelism but the practical scope is limited to single-node PP settings, with unclear gains at production scale
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Well-designed experiments across multiple schedules and models with proper baselines, though convergence theory relies on idealizations and some experiments are single-seed
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The DAG-based LP formulation for pipeline-aware freeze ratio optimization is a novel and elegant approach to an underexplored problem intersection
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Excellent presentation with clear figures, well-structured phases, and informative pipeline visualizations that build strong intuition

title: DocSeeker: Structured Visual Reasoning with Evidence Grounding for Long Document Understanding

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong empirical contribution with practical utility, particularly for RAG integration and long-document generalization, but relies on evidence annotations and proprietary models for training data
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine bottleneck in long document understanding with demonstrated practical synergy with RAG systems and strong generalization results
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Comprehensive ablation studies and well-controlled experiments, though reliance on proprietary teacher models and limited architecture diversity are concerns
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The ALR paradigm and EviGRPO are thoughtful combinations of existing ideas (structured CoT, GRPO, resolution allocation) rather than fundamentally new techniques
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures, thorough appendices, and systematic presentation of results; the training pipeline is well-documented

title: FuxiShuffle: An Adaptive and Resilient Shuffle Service for Distributed Data Processing on Alibaba Cloud

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong production systems paper with clear practical impact but limited novelty in individual techniques and small-scale experimental validation relative to claimed deployment scale
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine and important bottleneck in large-scale data processing with demonstrated production impact at Alibaba Cloud
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Well-designed ablation studies but experimental scale (20 nodes) is vastly smaller than production claims (100K+), baselines are asymmetrically optimized, and statistical rigor is lacking
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Individual techniques (adaptive mode selection, progressive scheduling, incremental recovery) are sensible but not fundamentally new; the novelty is in their integration and deployment
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear architecture diagrams and workflow descriptions, though some threshold choices lack justification

title: A derivation of the late-time volume law for local operator entanglement

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid analytical contribution filling a recognized gap, but the key assumption limits rigor and the result is partially anticipated by random circuit calculations
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an open question in quantum chaos diagnostics with relevance to simulability and thermalization, but the incomplete justification of the central assumption limits the significance of the conclusion
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Careful derivation with transparent assumptions and systematic numerics, but the third assumption (Haar-random eigenstates) is not justified in the setting where LOE is defined, and system sizes are small
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The Liouvillian basis approach and combination of ETH with Weingarten calculus for LOE is new, but the strategy of replacing eigenstates with random states is well-established and the final result closely parallels known random circuit results
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with explicit assumptions, detailed appendices, and systematic numerical presentation; notation is heavy but manageable

title: On the complementary roles of anisotropic crack density and anisotropic crack driving force in phase-field modeling of mixed-mode fracture

new_ratings.score_reason: Useful parametric study with clear physical insights, but limited by lack of experimental validation for anisotropic cases and incremental methodology building on existing framework
new_ratings.significance_reason: Provides practical guidance for parameter calibration in anisotropic phase-field models, but findings are specific to one formulation and lack experimental confirmation
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Thermodynamically consistent formulation with systematic parametric design, but no anisotropic experimental validation, acknowledged artifacts affecting quantitative results, and fixed interaction exponents despite claiming tunability
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The framework is from prior work; the contribution is a parametric study and physical interpretation rather than a new method, though the geometry-dependent role expansion finding is insightful
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures, systematic presentation of parametric studies, and honest discussion of limitations; notation is thorough though dense

title: RAD-LAD: Rule and Language Grounded Autonomous Driving in Real-Time

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid systems contribution demonstrating real-time MLLM planning feasibility with useful diagnostic insights about benchmark difficulty, but limited by modest hybrid gains and standalone LAD performance gaps
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the critical latency bottleneck for language-based driving planners and provides valuable insight about geometric vs. semantic difficulty in benchmarks
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Good ablation coverage for both components, but LAD's Val14 underperformance, synthetic data quality concerns, and missing baselines on InterPlan weaken claims
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Interruptible inference architecture is a clean design innovation; RAD modifications are individually incremental though collectively effective; hybrid integration is straightforward
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures and thorough appendix including extended Q&A discussion, though the main text tries to cover too many contributions

title: Training Time Prediction for Mixed Precision-based Distributed Training

new_ratings.score_reason: Valid observation about precision-aware prediction, but incremental contribution with narrow evaluation on a single model and hardware setup
new_ratings.significance_reason: Identifies a real gap in training time prediction, but the solution is straightforward profiling at correct precision rather than a deeper contribution
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Single model/hardware evaluation, no ablations, simplified communication model, and potentially unfair baseline comparison significantly weaken the experimental evidence
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The core idea—profile operators at their actual precision—is relatively obvious once the problem is identified; limited algorithmic or theoretical novelty
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Paper is clearly written and well-structured for its short format, but many important details are omitted and figures could be more informative

title: NFTDELTA: Detecting Permission Control Vulnerabilities in NFT Contracts through Multi-View Learning

new_ratings.score_reason: Useful vulnerability taxonomy but core technical contribution (similarity detection) shows weak performance; misleading presentation of results
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real problem in NFT security with a practical taxonomy, but the detection framework's limited effectiveness reduces practical significance
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Misleading conflation of static analysis and similarity detection metrics; tiny comparison dataset (15 contracts); similarity module achieves only ~39% precision
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel vulnerability taxonomy for NFT contracts and multi-view fusion approach, but individual components (Word2Vec, Performer, FastGAT, similarity search) are established techniques
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally well-structured with clear figures and examples, but the presentation misleadingly emphasizes static analysis metrics as the framework's overall performance

title: Encoding Numerical Data for Generative Quantum Machine Learning

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses a real but relatively narrow issue in generative QML with a clean, zero-overhead solution, though limited experimental scope and 1D-only evaluation constrain impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Fills a genuine gap in the QCBM literature regarding numerical data encoding, but the practical impact is limited by the simplicity of tested scenarios and lack of multi-dimensional experiments
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Sound theoretical argument and systematic 1D experiments with proper statistical reporting, but missing multi-dimensional validation and formal theoretical guarantees
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel application of Gray codes to generative QML with a well-articulated connection between encoding and entanglement requirements, though Gray codes themselves are well-established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear exposition, good figures, and logical progression from theory to experiments; notation and concepts are accessible

title: Surface ferrimagnetic order in RuO2 film

new_ratings.score_reason: Paradigm-shifting contribution to a major ongoing debate, with strong experimental and theoretical support, though some additional validation would be desirable
new_ratings.significance_reason: Directly challenges the canonical status of RuO₂ as a prototypical altermagnet and provides a unifying framework for contradictory experimental reports
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Well-designed spin-ARPES measurements and systematic DFT calculations, but limited by single growth condition and some unverified predictions
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Identifies a previously overlooked surface ferrimagnetic mechanism and provides the first coherent explanation for the RuO₂ magnetism controversy
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured narrative with clear figures and logical flow, though some details of DFT+U tests and surface fraction analysis are deferred to supplements

title: CMB constraints on dark matter-proton scattering: investigating prior-volume effects using profile likelihoods

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid methodological contribution with clear practical implications for dark matter constraints, though the core insight about prior-volume effects builds on established understanding and the constraints themselves are not the most competitive
new_ratings.significance_reason: Important for proper interpretation of DM-baryon scattering limits and contributes to the broader discussion of statistical methodology in cosmology, but the specific model constraints are not frontier-level
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Thorough and systematic comparison across multiple prior choices, proper convergence criteria, careful treatment of boundary effects, and transparent methodology throughout
new_ratings.novelty_reason: First profile-likelihood constraints on fractional DM-proton scattering and first simultaneous variation of all three new parameters, but the prior-volume effect concept is well-established from other contexts
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Exceptionally well-organized paper with clear figures, logical flow from theory to methods to results, and accessible explanation of both Bayesian and frequentist frameworks

title: HumDex:Humanoid Dexterous Manipulation Made Easy

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid systems contribution with practical value, but limited algorithmic novelty and insufficient validation of the generalization framework beyond one simple task
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine bottleneck in humanoid data collection; the portable low-cost system and open-source commitment could accelerate research, but generalization claims need broader validation
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Reasonable experimental coverage across five tasks but lacks statistical significance tests, has small sample sizes, and the generalization study is limited to one simple task
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Individual components (IMU tracking, MLP retargeting, sequential training) are technically straightforward; novelty is primarily in the system integration rather than algorithmic innovation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures, thorough appendix with hardware and training details, and good task descriptions; some notation could be more consistent

title: EPDQ: Efficient and Privacy-Preserving Exact Distance Query on Encrypted Graphs

new_ratings.score_reason: The paper addresses a relevant problem but has fundamental flaws in methodology, security analysis, and experimental evaluation with no baseline comparisons
new_ratings.significance_reason: The problem is important but the contribution is undermined by lack of comparisons, impractical query times, and unsubstantiated claims about top-k support
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Security proofs are superficial with undefined leakage functions, algorithms contain errors, no baseline comparisons, and experimental results show impractical performance
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The tensor framing appears cosmetic with no genuine tensor operations; the underlying approach is a relatively standard combination of PLL with searchable encryption
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Notation inconsistencies, algorithm errors, mismatched references, and disconnect between claimed contributions (top-k) and actual technical content

title: Online Survival Analysis: A Bandit Approach under Cox PH Model

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid first contribution to an underexplored problem with clean theoretical guarantees, but limited by restrictive model assumptions and modest empirical evaluation
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine gap between survival analysis and online decision-making with clear practical applications in clinical trials and precision medicine
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Theoretical proofs are well-structured but rely on strong assumptions; empirical evaluation lacks competing baselines and uses circular oracle construction
new_ratings.novelty_reason: First work to formally integrate survival analysis with right censoring into a bandit framework with regret guarantees; the martingale adjustment technique is elegant
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear problem formulation and notation; the dual time-scale structure is explained effectively, though some proof details are dense

title: Partial oracles quantum algorithm framework -- Part I: Analysis of in-place operations

new_ratings.score_reason: Mathematically sound framework for a restricted case that admits no quantum advantage; all practical significance is deferred to future work
new_ratings.significance_reason: No quantum advantage is demonstrated; the problems solved are classically trivial, and the claimed potential for exponential speedup remains unsubstantiated
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Proofs for the restricted in-place case appear correct, but the framework's applicability to hard problems is entirely unproven
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The reciprocal transform and chain rule are novel constructs, but their utility is unclear without extension to out-of-place operations
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear visualizations and honest discussion of limitations, though notation is heavy in places

title: Don't Let the Claw Grip Your Hand: A Security Analysis and Defense Framework for OpenClaw

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses an important problem but lacks scientific novelty, methodological rigor, and uses questionable model versions that undermine credibility
new_ratings.significance_reason: Important problem space but contributions are incremental — confirms known vulnerabilities and proposes standard engineering defenses
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Single-run evaluations without statistical analysis, references to non-existent model versions, no comparison with alternative defenses
new_ratings.novelty_reason: HITL defense is a combination of well-known techniques (regex filtering, allowlists, human approval); attack taxonomy follows existing MITRE frameworks
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures and tables, but insufficient detail on the semantic judge layer and defense implementation specifics

title: Dismagicker: Unitary Gate for Non-Stabilizerness Reduction

new_ratings.score_reason: Interesting conceptual contribution that fills a recognized gap, but limited numerical evidence and underdeveloped optimization methodology constrain the practical impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Introduces a useful concept bridging entanglement and non-stabilizerness management, but the practical benefits demonstrated are modest and system scales are small
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Limited numerical experiments (6-qubit random states, 20-site Heisenberg chain), ad hoc optimization strategies, and acknowledged but unresolved optimization failures
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The conceptual framing of dismagickers as counterparts to disentanglers is novel and intuitive, though the underlying techniques (variational optimization of non-Clifford gates) are relatively standard
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-written and clearly structured for a short paper, though the optimization methodology could be described more precisely and the limitations discussed more thoroughly

title: Predicting massive helium-3 release from metal tritides using simple mechanical modeling

new_ratings.score_reason: Clever mechanistic insight with practical importance for tritium safety, but limited validation scope and depth of analysis
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important practical problem in nuclear safety with a predictive framework that could reduce reliance on decades-long experiments
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Good agreement with PdTx data but lacks sensitivity analysis, uncertainty quantification, and validation on other tritide systems
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel and physically intuitive mechanism connecting precipitation hardening concepts to helium retention thresholds; no prior mechanistic explanation existed
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Core idea is well communicated but the paper is terse in critical implementation details and contains some notation inconsistencies

title: Bridging Expectation Signals: LLM-Based Experiments and a Behavioral Kalman Filter Framework

new_ratings.score_reason: Timely topic with a clear research question, but overclaimed theoretical framework, narrow experimental design, and notable methodological shortcomings substantially limit impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a relevant emerging question but findings are preliminary and narrowly validated, limiting downstream utility
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Weak mapping between theoretical framework and estimation, single shock magnitude, no robustness checks, questionable citations, and insufficient controls
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Combining Kalman Filter framing with LLM expectation experiments is somewhat novel, but the actual regression methodology is standard and the BKF adds little beyond notation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally readable structure but contains typos, inconsistent notation, incorrect citations, and a gap between the theoretical presentation and actual empirical work

title: Seeking Help, Facing Harm: Auditing TikTok's Mental Health Recommendations

new_ratings.score_reason: Timely and well-designed audit study with policy relevance, but limited by moderate classifier performance, small sample sizes, and lack of formal statistical analysis
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important and timely intersection of platform safety and mental health, with clear policy implications, though findings partially confirm expected patterns
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Creative experimental design but undermined by moderate classifier accuracy (~64% F1 for key labels), small validation sample, minimal statistical testing, and only 5 accounts per condition
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel LLM-agent audit methodology and the intent-vs-behavior factorial design are genuine contributions; the help-seeking paradox framing is compelling though the underlying finding is somewhat expected
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and thorough appendices, though the main text could better address statistical evidence for its claims

title: Facet-dependent Chemical Kinetics Governed Growth of Twisted Graphene Layers with Pre-designed Angles

new_ratings.score_reason: Major contribution addressing a critical bottleneck in twistronics with a novel mechanistic framework and experimental validation, though lacking reproducibility statistics and transport measurements
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the urgent need for scalable magic-angle TGL synthesis, potentially transforming access to twistronic materials for the condensed matter community
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Exceptional multi-technique in situ characterization and DFT support, but missing statistical reproducibility data and transport validation
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel facet-pairing strategy for twist-angle control via dynamic substrate reconstruction is a conceptually new approach distinct from all prior methods
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured logical progression from fundamentals to application, though the dense multi-panel figures and long paper could benefit from more concise presentation

title: Fermionic entanglement and quantum correlation measures in molecules

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid theoretical contribution with new measures and thorough analysis, but limited to a single small molecule with minimal basis, reducing demonstrated impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Introduces useful diagnostic tools at the QIT-QC interface, but practical utility beyond characterization is not demonstrated
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Careful proofs of formal properties, exact FCI calculations, and analytical dissociation limits; limited only by basis set size
new_ratings.novelty_reason: New negativity measures and mutual information variants for fermionic spin sectors are genuinely novel, though they build incrementally on established frameworks
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear notation and logical flow, though the density of formalism in Section II may challenge non-specialist readers

title: RobotPan: A 360$^\circ$ Surround-View Robotic Vision System for Embodied Perception

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong systems contribution combining hardware, algorithms, and dataset for an important practical problem, but limited novelty in core components and missing real-world deployment validation
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine and timely need in robotic teleoperation and embodied perception, with a practical dataset contribution
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Solid experimental comparisons on multiple benchmarks with good ablations, but streaming evaluation is small-scale and real-time claims lack end-to-end latency validation
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Spherical voxel hierarchy is a clean contribution, but geometry backbone heavily borrows from VGGT/π3 and streaming components use established techniques
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and mathematical formulations, though dense and covering many components

title: Spin-Flip Configuration Interaction for Strong Static Correlation in Quantum Electrodynamics

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid methodological extension with clear derivation but limited numerical validation and entirely prospective applications
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real gap at the intersection of strong correlation and cavity QED, but impact is limited by toy-system demonstrations and speculative applications
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Clean derivations but no validation against higher-level QED methods, only two trivial test systems, and acknowledged spin contamination issues left unresolved
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The combination of spin-flip methods with QED is new and conceptually appealing, though the individual components (SF-CIS, Pauli-Fierz Hamiltonian) are well-established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear equations and figures; the Hamiltonian matrix presentation is particularly transparent

title: Fractional-Time Jaynes-Cummings Model: Unitary Description of its Quantum Dynamics, Inverse Problem and Photon Statistics

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid theoretical exploration of a novel framework applied to a canonical model, with interesting phenomenological findings but limited physical motivation and analytical depth
new_ratings.significance_reason: Identifies interesting dynamical regimes (α=0.50 transition) and provides IPA for experimental simulation, but the physical relevance of fractional-time evolution in cavity QED remains unclear
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Mathematically consistent framework with proper unitarity restoration and well-executed numerics, but limited parameter space exploration and missing convergence analysis
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Extends a recently proposed unitary framework to photon statistics analysis and introduces the IPA interpretation, though the underlying framework is from prior work
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures, logical flow, and complete appendices enabling reproducibility; notation is consistent throughout

title: GuardPhish: Securing Open-Source LLMs from Phishing Abuse

new_ratings.score_reason: Useful dataset and conceptual framing of enforcement gap, but undermined by small evaluation sample, weak ASR methodology, and incremental defense contribution
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real and timely problem in offline LLM safety, but limited evaluation depth reduces the weight of the central claims
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Core enforcement gap evaluation uses only 1.14% of the dataset with undefined ASR criteria; classifier evaluation lacks adversarial robustness testing
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The enforcement gap concept is a useful contribution and multi-vector phishing coverage is new, but the defense methodology is standard fine-tuning
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear taxonomy and pipeline description, though key methodological details (ASR criteria, sampling justification) are missing

title: A Case Study on the Impact of Anonymization Along the RAG Pipeline

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses a practical and timely question with reasonable experimental breadth, but limited by unvalidated evaluation methodology and workshop-level depth
new_ratings.significance_reason: Novel question about mitigation placement in RAG is practically relevant, but findings are preliminary and configuration-specific
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Comprehensive experimental grid but critical privacy metric is unvalidated, no statistical tests, and confounds between retrieval and generation effects are not controlled
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The placement question is genuinely new and the systematic comparison is valuable, though the methods and metrics used are all existing
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with detailed prompts, clear figures, and comprehensive result tables; the appendix examples are particularly helpful

title: Atomistic Mechanisms of Temperature-Dependent Ion Track Formation in Gallium Nitride under Swift Heavy Ion Irradiation

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses a relevant problem with some novel findings but lacks methodological transparency, thorough validation, and depth of analysis
new_ratings.significance_reason: Practically relevant to GaN radiation hardening with novel observations of zincblende nanodomains, but device-level implications remain speculative
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Key methodological details (interatomic potential, coupling parameters) omitted from main text; limited statistical analysis and experimental validation
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Temperature-dependent morphological transition mapping and zincblende nanodomain discovery are novel, though TTM-MD methodology is well-established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Main text is brief with key details in supplementary materials; formatting issues present but overall logical flow is maintained

title: Bellman Value Decomposition for Task Logic in Safe Optimal Control

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong theoretical contribution with novel Bellman decomposition results and practical algorithm, validated on hardware, though limited predicate class and incomplete continuous-space guarantees temper the impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the fundamental gap between TL specification and RL optimization with a principled value decomposition that avoids reward tuning and enables automatic safety-liveness balancing
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Extensive proofs with contraction guarantees, convergence analysis, and policy synthesis results; minor gaps in continuous state-space guarantees acknowledged
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The value-function algebra concept is genuinely novel, the RAℓ Bellman equation is new, and the overall decomposition framework significantly generalizes prior work
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear figures and thorough appendix, though the density of notation and the number of lemmas/theorems makes the main narrative somewhat hard to follow

title: Effect of reaction temperature on nascent carbonaceous particles from toluene shock-tube pyrolysis: Insights from FTIR and Raman spectroscopy

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid experimental contribution with novel FTIR deconvolution methodology and systematic temperature mapping, but limited by indirect radical inference and single-campaign statistics
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an active and important question in soot inception with results that complement recent high-profile radical-mediated inception studies
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Thorough sensitivity analysis and artifact controls, but weakened by separate measurement campaigns, single experiments per condition, and inferential radical assignments
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel FTIR C=C deconvolution and temperature-resolved mapping in SPST are valuable but represent incremental advances over existing spectroscopic approaches
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and logical progression from observables to interpretation, though the dense spectral analysis sections could benefit from streamlining

title: Cloud-native and Distributed Systems for Efficient and Scalable Large Language Models -- A Research Agenda

new_ratings.score_reason: Broad survey/vision paper covering well-trodden ground without novel contributions, empirical validation, or sufficiently deep analysis to drive the field forward
new_ratings.significance_reason: Covers an important topic area but provides limited actionable insights beyond what is already known to active researchers in the field
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Consistent structural template but shallow analysis across most subsections, no empirical validation, and no systematic methodology for identifying research gaps
new_ratings.novelty_reason: No new systems, abstractions, or empirical findings; the organizational contribution overlaps substantially with existing surveys in this rapidly evolving space
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally well-organized with useful tables and figures, but inconsistent writing quality across sections and excessive breadth dilutes clarity of the core message

title: PRIME: Training Free Proactive Reasoning via Iterative Memory Evolution for User-Centric Agent

new_ratings.score_reason: Practical framework with clear motivation but overstated claims, missing ablations, incomplete baselines, and limited novelty over existing memory/experience-based approaches
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real practical need for gradient-free agent improvement, but the substantial performance gap with RL methods limits impact
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Missing key ablations, absent stated baselines (Reflexion, ReAct), broken figure references, hidden GPT-4o costs, and overstated competitive claims
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Integration of existing ideas (RAG, experience replay, evolutionary operators) into a coherent framework, but individual components are well-established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally well-written with helpful figures and examples, but marred by broken references and incomplete experimental details typical of premature submission

title: Physics-Informed Policy Optimization via Analytic Dynamics Regularization

new_ratings.score_reason: Sound idea with clean formulation but insufficient experimental validation on simple benchmarks with missing critical baselines and no real-world validation
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real problem in RL for robotics but limited to simulation-only settings and demonstrated only on simple tasks
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Missing comparisons with physics-informed baselines, no ablation studies, no statistical significance tests, and PINN accuracy unvalidated
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Reasonable combination of Lagrangian mechanics with policy gradient regularization, though conceptually similar to prior soft-constraint and residual approaches
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear mathematical exposition, but overclaims relative to evidence and some notation inconsistencies

title: Breaking the Training Barrier of Billion-Parameter Universal Machine Learning Interatomic Potentials

new_ratings.score_reason: Excellent systems engineering contribution that solves a genuine infrastructure bottleneck, but incomplete scientific validation of the billion-parameter models limits overall impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a critical scaling bottleneck for the rapidly growing uMLIP field; infrastructure enables fundamentally faster iteration cycles for foundation model development in atomistic simulation
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Thorough performance evaluation across two systems with proper scaling studies, but accuracy evaluation limited to the smallest model variant; the key scientific claim about billion-parameter benefits remains unsupported
new_ratings.novelty_reason: First hybrid-parallel training framework for second-order uMLIPs; novel JIT sparse expert planning and element-type MoE routing are well-motivated architectural innovations
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear figures and systematic presentation of optimizations, though the paper is dense with many interleaved contributions that can be difficult to disentangle

title: Aligning Dense Retrievers with LLM Utility via DistillationAligning Dense Retrievers with LLM Utility via Distillation

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid practical contribution addressing an important RAG bottleneck, but limited evaluation scope and insufficient ablations weaken the overall impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the important efficiency-quality tradeoff in RAG retrieval with demonstrated practical benefits and composability
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Two-stage approach is well-motivated but evaluation limited to two datasets, one generator, no significance tests, and missing key ablations
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The utility-based distribution matching formulation is a reasonable extension of existing cross-encoder-to-bi-encoder distillation, with utility-gated mining as a novel component
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and framework description, though zero-shot results lack detail and some experimental choices are under-explained

title: Scalable phonon-laser arrays with self-organized synchronization

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid theoretical proposal with clear novelty in architecture, but limited by mean-field approximations, modest scalability demonstration, and lack of experimental validation
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real limitation in phonon laser scalability with a modular approach, but practical impact awaits experimental demonstration
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Careful derivations with multiple validation checks, but mean-field decoupling and limited robustness analysis weaken quantitative reliability for large systems
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The local-driving architecture for individually addressable phonon laser arrays is genuinely new, though it combines established theoretical tools
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured presentation with clear figures, detailed supplementary derivations, and logical progression from minimal to many-body system

title: Continuous Quantum Aperture: Beamforming with a Single-Vapor-Cell Rydberg Receiver

new_ratings.score_reason: Novel concept with strong theoretical and experimental support, but practical applicability remains distant due to low data rates and controlled conditions
new_ratings.significance_reason: Introduces a fundamentally new beamforming mechanism that bridges quantum physics and antenna theory, with clear implications for multiple application domains
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Extensive experimental validation with 100+ patterns and good theory-experiment agreement, though fitting parameters in multipeak case and low data rates limit some claims
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The continuous quantum aperture concept is genuinely new, transforming single vapor cells from omnidirectional sensors to directional receivers through a previously unrecognized mechanism
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and logical flow from theory to experiment to applications, though some mathematical steps require supplementary material

title: Safe Navigation in Unknown and Cluttered Environments via Direction-Aware Convex Free-Region Generation

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid incremental contribution to convex free-region navigation with useful direction-awareness and continuous safety, but limited experimental scope and no completeness guarantees
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses real limitations of existing free-region methods in cluttered environments, though the practical impact is constrained by assumptions on convex robot geometry and lack of dynamic feasibility
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Sound mathematical formulation but limited quantitative evaluation scope (small environments, homogeneous obstacles, no 3D baselines, small trial counts without statistical tests)
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Direction-biased QP for region generation and Lipschitz-based continuous safety are meaningful but incremental advances over existing convex free-region and safety verification methods
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear figures and algorithm description; mathematical notation is precise and the motivation is effectively communicated

title: Cooperate to Compete: Strategic Data Generation and Incentivization Framework for Coopetitive Cross-Silo Federated Learning

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid theoretical framework addressing a relevant gap, but limited by synthetic experiments, simplified assumptions, and narrow baseline comparisons
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important and underexplored problem in cross-silo FL, but practical applicability is constrained by strong informational assumptions
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Game-theoretic analysis is technically sound with convergence proofs, but relies on empirically fit scaling laws and exogenous competition parameters without robustness analysis
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel integration of coopetition modeling, strategic GenAI data generation, and payoff redistribution in FL; the weighted potential game formulation is a meaningful contribution
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear workflow diagrams and comprehensive notation, though dense and with heavy notation overhead that may limit accessibility

title: Structural Dependency Analysis for Masked NTT Hardware: Scalable Pre-Silicon Verification of Post-Quantum Cryptographic Accelerators

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid engineering contribution addressing a real scalability gap in PQC hardware verification, but limited algorithmic novelty and significant methodological gaps (no glitch model, incomplete coverage of largest modules)
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine bottleneck in PQC hardware certification with practical tooling, though the sound-but-incomplete nature limits its standalone utility
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Sound within stated assumptions with good validation infrastructure, but standard probing model limitation is severe for real hardware, and the headline Barrett result lacks direct cross-tool validation
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Label propagation and fixed-point iteration are standard techniques; arithmetic SADC reparametrization is novel but conceptually straightforward; primary contribution is scale demonstration rather than algorithmic innovation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Excessively long and highly repetitive—key results are restated 5+ times; the paper would benefit enormously from halving its length while preserving the same technical content

title: Analyzing Vector Register Usage in Linux Packages to Understand Real-World Impact of Downfall Attack

new_ratings.score_reason: Descriptive measurement study with limited security depth; presence of vector instructions is a very weak proxy for actual Downfall vulnerability
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real gap but findings are too coarse to drive actionable security decisions or advance the field meaningfully
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Large-scale static analysis is thorough but lacks runtime validation; equating instruction presence with vulnerability is a major methodological weakness
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Methodology is standard binary analysis applied to a new context; no new techniques, attacks, or defenses proposed
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Paper is well-structured and clearly written with good figures, though security implications are overstated relative to evidence

title: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Maximizing Quantum Fisher Information in Time-Dependent Many-Body Systems

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid proof-of-concept with careful methodology but limited by small system sizes, lack of comparison with established methods, and scalability constraints that reduce practical impact
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important problem at the intersection of quantum metrology and ML, but the practical advantage over existing methods at accessible scales is not demonstrated
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Well-constructed methodology with proper error analysis and honest limitation discussions, but critically lacking comparisons against established quantum control methods
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel combination of PINN framework with counter-diabatic driving for QFI maximization, including trainable scheduling and Magnus propagation, though individual components are known
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Thorough but overly lengthy exposition with redundancy; the paper would benefit from tighter writing, though the mathematical framework is clearly presented

title: Generative Channel Knowledge Base With Environmental Information for Joint Source-Channel Coding in Semantic Communications

new_ratings.score_reason: Interesting conceptual framework but severely limited by small-scale simulation-only validation with insufficient baselines
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine gap in environment-aware SemCom, but practical significance is limited by narrow experimental scope
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Only 995 samples, single scenario, simulation-only channels, low-resolution images, and limited baselines undermine empirical claims
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The CKB concept bridging environmental features to channel knowledge for SemCom is a fresh and relevant idea, though individual components are standard
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Generally well-structured but notation inconsistencies, hard-to-read figures, and some redundant exposition reduce readability

title: Tube Diffusion Policy: Reactive Visual-Tactile Policy Learning for Contact-rich Manipulation

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong conceptual contribution bridging diffusion policies with feedback control, with solid empirical results, but limited by assumptions in theoretical analysis and moderate real-world validation scale
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a fundamental limitation of action chunking in diffusion policies with a generalizable framework applicable beyond the specific tasks demonstrated
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Clean theoretical derivation but relies on restrictive assumptions (Lx<1, isotropic contraction); real-world experiments are limited to 25 episodes per task with no statistical significance testing
