new_ratings.novelty_reason: The action tube concept and dual-time formulation are novel combinations of ideas from tube MPC, diffusion policies, and streaming flows, though individual components draw heavily from prior work
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear exposition of the framework, good figures, and thorough algorithmic pseudocode; the connection between theory and practice is well articulated

title: Test of the essential collapse-locality loophole

new_ratings.score_reason: Addresses a genuine loophole with a clever experimental concept, but limited by testing only the weaker form, moderate precision, and informal error analysis
new_ratings.significance_reason: Tests a real but niche loophole in quantum foundations; only addresses the essential (weaker) version, limiting broader impact
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Clever experimental design but uncertainty analysis is informal, systematic errors drawn from specs rather than calibrated, and key caveats not fully resolved
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The variable-distance approach with classical reference signals is a genuinely novel strategy for addressing the collapse-locality loophole model-independently
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Conceptual argument is clearly presented with good figures; supplementary material provides useful detail, though the main text could be tighter

title: An accurate theoretical framework for the optical and electronic properties of paracyclophanes

new_ratings.score_reason: Solid methodological contribution with excellent validation, but more incremental than transformative; limited to homo-PCPs with extensions to hetero-PCPs promised but not delivered.
new_ratings.significance_reason: Fills a real gap in quantitative modeling of PCP properties with clear design implications, though the immediate impact is somewhat niche within the cyclophane/organic electronics community.
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Exceptional multi-property validation (spectra, redox, TAS) across diverse systems with systematic comparison of supermolecular and fragment approaches; minor issues with reorganization energy overestimation acknowledged.
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The CC2/TD-DFT combination and Frenkel exciton validation for PCPs are useful but represent a careful application and combination of existing methods rather than fundamentally new methodology.
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and systematic presentation; some density in the theoretical methods section but overall accessible to the target audience.

title: RankUp: Towards High-rank Representations for Large Scale Advertising Recommender Systems

new_ratings.score_reason: Strong industrial deployment with significant business impact, but limited novelty in individual components and no reproducible evaluation on public benchmarks
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an important gap between parameter scaling and representation quality in large-scale recommenders, with clear production impact
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Good empirical analysis of effective rank dynamics but lacks theoretical grounding, public benchmarks, and statistical significance testing for ablations
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Individual components are largely known techniques; novelty lies in their combination under the effective rank framework, which is primarily an engineering contribution
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and ablation organization, though the connection between offline metrics and online GMV gains could be better explained

title: StrEBM: A Structured Latent Energy-Based Model for Blind Source Separation

new_ratings.score_reason: Early-stage conceptual exploration with no baselines, no real-world experiments, no theory, and limited empirical validation on a single toy problem
new_ratings.significance_reason: The per-dimension energy idea is interesting but not clearly differentiated from existing structured prior approaches, and no practical impact is demonstrated
new_ratings.rigor_reason: No baselines, no standard metrics, no theoretical analysis, toy-scale experiments only, and nonlinear results show instability
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Source-wise learnable energies in an EBM framework is a somewhat novel framing, though closely related to per-dimension GP priors in existing work
new_ratings.clarity_reason: The mathematical formulation is clear and the algorithm is well-specified, but the paper is excessively verbose with significant redundancy across sections

title: Real-Time GPU-Accelerated Monte Carlo Evaluation of Safety-Critical AEB Systems Under Uncertainty

new_ratings.score_reason: Competent engineering demonstration with clear regulatory motivation, but limited novelty in computational approach and oversimplified vehicle model for safety-critical claims
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a real regulatory need but the gap between this proof-of-concept and production-ready uncertainty-aware AEB remains substantial
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Cross-platform evaluation is thorough and numerical consistency is well-validated, but timing budget assumptions are questionable and no comparison with alternative UQ methods is provided
new_ratings.novelty_reason: GPU-parallelized Monte Carlo is well-established; the contribution is primarily applying known techniques to a specific domain with no methodological innovation
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear exposition, thorough related work, and good figures, though some claims (high-fidelity, real-time deployable) are overstated

title: RAAP: Retrieval-Augmented Affordance Prediction with Cross-Image Action Alignment

new_ratings.score_reason: A solid contribution combining retrieval and learning for affordance prediction with good real-world validation, but limited task scope and narrow baseline comparisons
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the practical bottleneck of data-efficient affordance generalization with a principled decomposition, though limited to short-horizon single-step tasks
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Multi-setting evaluation with ablations is commendable, but limited baselines, small real-world trial counts without statistical tests, and missing static affordance evaluation are notable gaps
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The static/dynamic affordance decomposition with complementary inference mechanisms is a novel and well-motivated design choice, though individual components are established
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and method description; the pipeline is easy to follow and the experimental setup is well-documented

title: A digitally controlled silicon quantum processing unit

new_ratings.score_reason: Landmark system-level integration of silicon spin qubits with cryogenic control, demonstrating order-of-magnitude performance improvements and error correction protocols with a scalable architecture
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses the critical wiring/control bottleneck for scaling semiconductor qubits and demonstrates a complete QPU architecture with clear path to commercial relevance
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Exceptionally thorough multi-level characterization with simulation-experiment comparison, though reliance on ersatz miscalibration parameter and unexplained measurement spikes indicate gaps
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Novel system-level integration of cryogenic CMOS controller, superconducting ribbon cable, and EO qubits; individual components build on known approaches but their co-design and integration is new
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured main text with excellent figures; the extensive 40-page supplement is comprehensive but makes the full picture difficult to grasp without significant effort

title: Consistency of AI-Generated Exercise Prescriptions: A Repeated Generation Study Using a Large Language Model

new_ratings.score_reason: Preliminary single-model descriptive study with reasonable framework but limited novelty, no expert validation, and findings that largely confirm expected LLM behavior
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses an underexplored dimension (consistency) but findings are unsurprising and no solutions are proposed; clinical impact is minimal without validity assessment
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Appropriate statistical methods and controlled generation, but critically lacks expert validation of AI-judge classifications and uses only a single model at maximum temperature
new_ratings.novelty_reason: The three-dimensional evaluation framework for exercise prescription consistency is a reasonable contribution, but the approach largely applies known NLP evaluation techniques to a new domain
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-organized paper with clear tables, figures, and systematic presentation of methods and results; writing is accessible despite dense statistical content

title: RadarSplat-RIO: Indoor Radar-Inertial Odometry with Gaussian Splatting-Based Radar Bundle Adjustment

new_ratings.score_reason: Novel first-of-kind radar BA framework with strong conceptual contribution, but limited by small-scale evaluation, single baseline comparison, and 3-DoF restriction
new_ratings.significance_reason: Opens a new direction for radar SLAM by introducing BA, but practical impact is limited by 3-DoF constraint and narrow evaluation scope
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Ablations are good but evaluation uses only 5 custom sequences, 1 baseline, pseudo-ground truth of uncertain quality, and no runtime analysis
new_ratings.novelty_reason: First radar bundle adjustment framework and first full RAD Gaussian Splatting rendering are genuinely novel contributions that bridge an identified gap
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured paper with clear figures and pipeline description, though some implementation details (e.g., optimization hyperparameters, convergence behavior) are underspecified

title: Closing the ultrahigh temperature metrology gap: non-contact thermal conductivity ($\mathrm{k}$) and spectral emittance ($\mathrm{\varepsilon_λ}$) of molybdenum up to 3200 K

new_ratings.score_reason: Well-executed instrumentation/metrology advance addressing a real measurement gap, but limited to a single benchmark material and incremental over the group's prior PRL demonstration
new_ratings.significance_reason: Addresses a genuine data gap in ultrahigh-temperature thermal conductivity relevant to hypersonics, fusion, and AM, though the immediate dataset (Mo only) has limited novelty
new_ratings.rigor_reason: Comprehensive uncertainty propagation, sensitivity analysis, and bounding studies with transparent reporting; among the most rigorous UHT metrology papers
new_ratings.novelty_reason: Incremental improvement over the group's prior SSTDR demonstration; the individual components (lock-in thermography, hyperspectral pyrometry, FEM inversion) are established techniques combined in a new configuration
new_ratings.clarity_reason: Well-structured with clear figures and systematic presentation, though the paper is dense and the edge correction methodology could benefit from more intuitive explanation
